creator: vwe@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 11/10/11 09:18, Darren Pilgrim wrote:
> Yes, I'm aware, thanks. The commit that introduced the error has been
> reverted. This PR can be closed.

Dear Darren,

I'm sorry to tell but I resist to close this PR as according to cvsweb 
(yes, I know cvsweb isn't authoritative) files/postgrey.in is still at 
rev 1.6, last modified at 2011-11-09, and hasn't been fixed yet.

I can see, there has been a commit changing some of your ports' files 
some hours back, but the issue is still there. I was able to confirm 
cvsweb output by doing a fresh checkout of the ports tree.

On a personal note, I do not recommend to roll back a bigger commit just 
to fix a minor problem. If a commit introduces a new problem, fixing 
just the new problem would be an appropriate action IMHO. YMMV but 
everything else makes the commit log harder to read and understand.

Regards,

Volker Werth
